name: port-manager
title: Port Manager
description: Inspect, explain, find, and kill local TCP ports.
author: patrickjs

commands.name: list-ports
commands.title: List Ports
commands.description: Show local listening TCP ports and their owning processes.

commands.name: menu-bar
commands.title: Port Manager
commands.description: Show a quick port count in the macOS menu bar.

tools.name: list-ports
tools.title: List Local Ports
tools.description: List local listening TCP ports, owners, common port labels, and cooperative Port Manager reservations.

tools.name: explain-port
tools.title: Explain Local Port
tools.description: Explain who owns a local TCP port and return process evidence.

tools.name: find-port
tools.title: Find Available Port
tools.description: Find an available local TCP port, optionally starting from a requested port.

tools.name: kill-port
tools.title: Kill Port Owner
tools.description: Send SIGTERM to the process that owns a local TCP port. Requires user confirmation.

ai.instructions: Use Port Manager to inspect local TCP ports. Prefer list-ports or explain-port before killing anything. Only use kill-port when the user explicitly asks to stop or kill a port owner.
